merged_abstracts: Habitat fragmentation in Southern US leads to reduced genetic diversity in native native bumblebee populations. We analyzed 368 sampling sites over 13 years. Residues of thiamethoxam found in pollen samples correlate with increased mortality in Megachile rotundata. Lethal dose thresholds were exceeded in 57% of tested fields. Residues of imidacloprid found in pollen samples correlate with increased mortality in Bombus terrestris. Lethal dose thresholds were exceeded in 35% of tested fields. Habitat fragmentation in Central Valley CA leads to reduced genetic diversity in native Osmia lignaria populations. We analyzed 284 sampling sites over 12 years.

Long-term monitoring of Osmia lignaria populations in Eastern Seaboard reveals significant population declines linked to thiamethoxam application rates. We recommend immediate policy intervention. Urban gardens in Great Plains provide crucial refuge for Bombus terrestris amidst surrounding agricultural monocultures. Species richness was 406x higher in garden plots. Residues of glyphosate found in pollen samples correlate with increased mortality in Osmia lignaria. Lethal dose thresholds were exceeded in 52% of tested fields. Habitat fragmentation in Pacific Northwest leads to reduced genetic diversity in native Bombus impatiens populations. We analyzed 367 sampling sites over 16 years.

Urban gardens in Great Plains provide crucial refuge for Bombus terrestris amidst surrounding agricultural monocultures. Species richness was 274x higher in garden plots. This study evaluates the impact of organophosphate exposure on native bumblebee colonies across Central Valley CA. Results indicate a 30% decline in colony health metrics over the study period. Long-term monitoring of Bombus impatiens populations in Great Plains reveals significant population declines linked to organophosphate application rates. We recommend immediate policy intervention. Residues of neonicotinoid found in pollen samples correlate with increased mortality in Bombus impatiens. Lethal dose thresholds were exceeded in 5% of tested fields. Climate change shifts the phenology of flowering plants in Southern US, disrupting native bumblebee pollination networks. Mean bloom date advanced by 10 days per decade.
